mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a p-value below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction and reduced factory utilization, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns during slowdown periods, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and total nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and PAYEMS growth rates yields a statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 60-day SPX direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor market strength and elevated consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 150.0 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses the 150,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate hikes during inflationary periods compress equity valuations and increase financing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS increases during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns with a statistical significance level of p less than 0.05.
